The last time I visited this place was in 2021, and now 2024 I’ve come back again. Since it was a Friday, which is part of the weekend in Johor, I pected the place to be fully occupied. To my surprise, it was completely empty—no one else was there! We had the whole place to ourselves, which made the perience even better.We started by cooking Indian banana leaf curry rice and enjoyed some delicio payasam for dessert. Later in the evening, we prepared Korean steamboat for dinner, played UNO cards, and even danced the night away. The room was pleasantly cold even without turning on the fan, and the linens were clean and fresh.The nt morning, we cooked breakfast and enjoyed the amazing waterfall shower—it was such a refreshing perience. The waterfall itself is beautiful and serene. For lunch, we cooked again, and during that time, we noticed a group of motorbikes and some Singaporean cars entering the area. They stayed for about an hour to shower and then left.We eventually left in the evening, feeling incredibly satisfied. This time, having the entire waterfall to ourselves made the trip unforgettable. It truly felt like the whole place belonged to ! Highly recommend this unique perience.

This campsite/waterfall is located in Segamat district, Johor. Nearest town from Takah Pengkoi is Bekok town. You can get supplies here before entering the oil palm plantation where the campsite is located. Bear in mind there is very limited phone signal at the campsite. To get there, it is most convenient to locate Sekolah Kebangsaan Kampung Kudong then go pass it till you find the Takah Pengkoi sign board on your left. Turn left, go straight and you will arrive at the entrance of the campsite. Register and pay for the entrance fee. There is a kak Ros there who does the registration. Fees cost RM40 per pax per night, or RM70 per pax for 2 nights or RM15 per pax for day trip. Operation hours 9am to 7pm. The campsite parking can fit in 20-30 cars. Otherwise jt do roadside parking and walk a bit to the river/campsite. Basic toilet and shower facilities are available (male/female separated). Do not pect luxury. But water is strong for shower and the flh works. The campsite is well maintained, no garbage strewn around,  no funny smell, not much mosquito, no wild dogs/cats, no monkeys. It is good. But watch out for squirrels they'll eat any fruit left unsupeised. There are still other insects that bite like mosquito though. Crowds peak on Friday and leave by late Saturday afternoon becae Johor starts to work on Sunday. Solar lights are placed nearby toilets, walkway and parking area. To see stars at night, walk further away from camp to avoid the lights. There are several spots where you can safely enjoy the waterfall cascades. Feel the water rh that massages your back. Get some nice photos. Water is relatively clear at the upper levels and lesser at the lower part of the river. Some areas are shallow and it is suitable for children. Camp right in front of the river. Awesome perience to relax while keeping an eye on your children and doing barbecue. There are lots of shade and  you can feel the cool temperature.

In Malacca, which flourished as a hub for intermediary trade, we visited the homes of the Baba-Nyonya community, descendants of Chinese immigrants and local Malaysians. These residences, built in the Peranakan architectural style, reflect both tax-saving designs (such as narrow facades) and feng shui principles.The buildings feature long, narrow layouts, skylights, and inner courtyards—distinct characteristics of Peranakan architecture that also reveal the clever adaptations of the people who once lived there. Touring these homes offers a glimpse into their way of life and a chance to imagine daily life in those times.

The Baba & Nyonya Heritage Meum is a treasured cultural heritage site in Malacca's heart. It authentically recreates the lives of the Peranakan Chinese, a group that emerged from the long-term integration of early Chinese immigrants with the local Malaysian community. Formerly the century-old residence of the Zeng family, the meum was converted into a meum in 1985, to systematically presee and display their unique architecture, furnishings, clothing, and everyday items. Guided tours offer insightful planations of the social structure, religio beliefs, and ctoms of the Baba and Nyonya people, providing valuable insights into and understanding Malaysia's diverse cultural landscape.

I was staying in sukau at the homestay similarly with this place, but it known Balai Kito homestay mtara haji Rangon. Ezall I probably meet him that time shown a good person, honestly and understand a travelers. Price RM30 + breakfast, RM50/person you meal. He can take you to the journey or river cruise with smile and full of knowledge about rainforests.

In Sukau we checked in at homestay “Balai Kito”, well sign posted and about 500m after the village mosk, following the road along the river.
The owner Ezall, living with his mother and brother in this 9-room hoe was very helpful in organizing the boat trips. We paid 80MR for the simple air-conditioned room with toilet / shower ensuite, including 3 meals. He charged  60MR/pax for a boat trip (2 hours each) of which we had two and 125MR/pax for the elephant spotting boat trip. The elephants were far away down stream. That’s why he charge  that much.
The first tailor-made journey started 4pm. We saw lots of animals: birds, a crocodile, lots of different monkeys, and a snake, lying in a tree. All together it lasted 2 ½ hours. Ezall spotted animals easily. Sometimes with the help of his boats man.
After that we had a lovely dinner, prepared by his mother. Ezall’s late-grandfather had taught him everything about the jungle when he was a kid. His late-grandfather mt have been a real bhman. He also fought the Japanese in the jungle in WW II.
The nt morning we left for the second boat trip at 6am. This time in another boat with another boats man on another part of the river (more up stream). This time there was also a short walk in the jungle included. Ezall’s knew his way, as there were no paths. He plained how to escape if attacked by an animal, plained about trees and other interesting stuff. We saw a lot of animals again, some different (e.g. eagles) as the previo day. We also saw an enormo crocodile swimming (about 4-5 meters long!). The journey lasted again at least 2 ½ hours. After arriving back at the homestay, we had our simple breakfast with delicio homemade pastries. At about 10am we drove ourselves to the Gomantong Caves, 25 minutes drive, which was interesting. We even spotted a Oran Utan with a baby! After hiking in the surroundings of the cave, we went back to have a tasteful lunch at 1pm.
At 2pm we had our third boat trip, with Ezall and a different boats man. Al the journeys at was Ezall, boatman, my son and I. This trip was aimed at elephant spotting. The supposedly were far away downstream. It took  2 hours boating to find them. A herd of elephants. Males, females, younger ones and a baby. We saw the baby getting attacked by the younger elephants, who tried to kill the baby! They were at the riverbank with lh vegetation and we were up-close. I found it a miracle that Ezall and his mate spotted them. I would never have. Even being so close. They said they could smell them and see the fresh footprints. It was amazing. I have seen elephants in Africa on different occasions, but never encounters this close. The natural setting was also very different. Impressive!
On our way back (another 2 hours by boat), we were so lucky to see a single elephant crossing the enormo river. Amazing!
I have to say that Ezall showed great respect to all the animals we spotted and to the natural environment in general. I would highly recommend staying at homestay Balai Kito and Ezall Rohaizad Mohd. He has a hawk for wild life spotting and he stops wherever you like. Ezall is entertaining and knows a lot about the area. He has interesting stories to tell. The food is good. The homestay is spacio and clean. Ezall does everything in his power to make you never forget the trip. The whole packages was very reasonable priced and although paying for 2-hour trips, it never bothered that they took longer becae of requests for several stops as we spotted where we came for.
Again, Ezall is highly recommended. You show up at Homestay Balai Kito or you contact him by phone +601 45587675 or email balaikito2000@gmail.com

Me and two other friends had a lovely stay at Balai Kito. We booked a 2 nights/3 days package with them and it was one of our best periences in Borneo. They picked  up at Sandakan, had lunch together with their family, did several boat trips to spot wild life, made local handycraft, played a traditional game with the family, danced on traditional mic, etc. Recommended!!

My hband and I stayed with Ahbam and his family for two nights. They were tremely friendly and welcoming. They had lots of activities planned for  and we really enjoyed our stay. We had to change our dates around and they were really helpful. We stayed in an air conditioned room to the side of their hoe and their door was always open. They provided a full programme of river trips, trekking, going to Gommantang caves, dancing and even showed  some work on reforestation that they are doing so we helped them plant some trees. Well worth a visit and they will change the schedule based on your interests.

Two friends and my partner and me showed up at this homestay at around 8pm after our actual accomoodation failed to accommodate  and we asked for available rooms. Luckily for  they had two double rooms availabe and we could move in immediately. The rooms were very friendly and clean and so was the whole hoe. For 30RM/night/person you got a room with ensuite bathroom and a nice breakfast.
The early morning river cruise (50RM/person) we undertook was great, although the boatman arrived half an hour later then scheduled which meant that our chances of seeing crocodiles were very low becae it was too late when we finally started the cruise.
Unfortunately we could only stay for one night and therefore didn't have much time to talk to the friendly and knowledgable owners of this guesthoe. I certainly recommend to take your time and stay for at least two nights to really get the chance to talk to the owners and the other villagers and get to know their culture and way of living a bit better.

We booked the 4Days-3Nights Package to stay with Mr. Bam and his family. As we are traveling on a small budget we are looking wisely on what to spend our money. And this was definetly worth it :) At the first day we were lucky becae it was the end of Rammadan and Mr. Bam took  with them to their family. They were all really welcoming and the food was so delicio! The accomodation was basic but totally fine. Keep in mind that you are living with a local family when you book a homestay and not a 5-star-lodge so you have to adjt to their standard of living. Whe stayed at a double room on the other side of the hoe. Becae I am allergic to cats it was better for  than staying with them. But Mr. Bam offered  to stay in their living room, too. The Food was great and most of the time we ate with them. The TV was switched on all the time, but I guess that is jt normal for him and his eight kids and felt like we were integrated in their daily life.
The activities were great fun. We learned a lot about their culture and the conseation efforts in the Kinabatangan area. One of our highlights was the traditional dancing and karaoke night at our last day. All the kids were participating and it felt like they had as much fun as we had. When we booked the package, we were a little bit afraid that it would feel arranged and forced, but that wasnt the case at all :). The best were definetly the river cruises!! We have seen so many amazing animals including the pygme elephants, rare hornbills, lots of monkeys and so on. We've even seen an orangutan,too but it was a bit far away. Keep in mind that the Kinabatangan river is not a zoo, so they can't garantee anything. We did the cruises with Mr. Bams son with no other tourists cept . He is not yet a professionell guide, but he grew up nt to the jungle so he can answer you a lot of questions. Even if it was not everything perfectly in time, it was well organized and we liked that it was not the perfectly arranged touristy perience.

Mr. Bam works at an orangutan research organization called HUTAN and does a lot of conseation projects in the village. For  it was so interesing to talk to him and get to know his perspective on palm oil plantations, tourism etc. He is eager to answer any questions you might have :))

All in all it was one of our best periences in Borneo and a very special chance to get to know the local way of living, the culture and also the problems of Sukau..

I accidentally booked this trip and tried to cancel a couple minutes later, but since it was in the non-refundable window, I couldn't get a refund. I understand the non-refundable policy, but I feel like there should be a grace period of at least an hour when it comes to things like this.

I did not get to perience much of the tour becae I promptly fell and broke my wrist.

The seice could not have been better or more compassionate. As soon as I determined that I could not go on becae of the pain, the guide called for the driver (Eddie in this case) to come and pick me up. Eddie took me to the local hospital and stayed with me the whole time (it all took a couple of hours).  When my credit card would not work, he paid with his own. When after consultation and X-ray the doctor  stated that the wait to see a specialist would be several hours and suggested I go to a private hospital in George Town, Eddie drove me back, recommended a few places and dropped me at one of them closer to my Airbnb.  Finally when I wanted to compensate him for his time and trouble, he would not accept any money. Before leaving me, he gave me his phone number  in case I needed future help.

Take the perience knowing you will be cared for in case of need.

A Beautiful Garden Marred by Mischievo Monkeys*I recently visited this stunning botanical garden, and it was truly a feast for the eyes. The variety of plants and flowers on display was breathtaking, and the garden's layout was well-designed and easy to navigate.Note that it has many many hiking trails, one of them is connected the Penang HillThe scenery was picturesque, with plenty of tranquil spots to sit and take in the surroundings. I particularly enjoyed the scenic views and the sounds of nature.However, my perience was slightly marred by the presence of aggressive monkeys. They were quite bold and stole food from visitors, which was frtrating. I would advise visitors to be cautio and keep a close eye on their belongings.Despite this issue, I would still recommend the garden to anyone who loves nature and botany. With some measures to manage the monkey population, this garden could easily become a 5-star destination. For now, I'll give it 4 stars.

A wonderfully natural and super lh tropical botanical garden.  There is NO  ENTRANCE FEE at the Penang tropical garden as well.Uniquely different from the equally cellent KL and Singapore tropical gardens.This garden is a more natural,  more lh, and not nearly as well  manicured garden  as either the KL garden or the Singapore gardens. That is precisely its charm!There are loads of trails  winding through vario displays and presentations. Walkers, hikers, and  joggers seem to enjoy the multitude of trails.Monkeys are EVERYWHERE.There are displays of herb gardens, orchid gardens,  fern displays, aeroid trails, and much more.The original gardens were established in 1884 from an old quarry site, under the supeision of Charles Curtis, who was the first superintendent, making it one of the oldest botanic gardens built by the British in a colonial settlement.Penang Botanic Gardens is part of Penang Hill Biosphere Resee, recognized by UNESCO as the third Biosphere Resee in Malaysia listed in the World Network of Biosphere Resee (WNBR).[2]Currently, 12-2024, there is construction of a cable car system taking place, which limits access at the very beginning of the road leading into the garden.Restrooms 🚻 are limited.Handicap doable in many areas. Some areas are paved roughly and might present issues for those who are  unsteady at walking..Final take...HIGHLY RECOMMENDED.Be prepared to gain elevation as this garden winds uphill through super lh tropical canyons.There is a restaurant seing fresh coconut near the entrance.  A total godsend after a day ploring these wonderful gardens.

The Penang Botanical Gardens is one of the most refreshing spots to escape into nature while still being close to the city. Chew Jetty, the largest and most famo of the historic Clan Jetties in George Town, Penang, is a fascinating UNESCO World Heritage site offering a unique glimpse into a traditional waterfront lifestyle. It is not a single floating structure, but a community of floating hoes—or, more accurately, stilt hoes—built entirely over the water on wooden piers. These rtic, interconnected wooden homes have been continuoly inhabited by descendants of the Chew clan since the 19th century, creating a living, breathing village seemingly spended between land and sea.Visitors can plore the long wooden walkway to witness this unique way of life. While primarily a residential area, the jetty has become a popular tourist attraction, with many ground-level rooms in the hoes converted into small shops selling souvenirs and local treats.For those interested in the water perspective, boating is a popular activity. Tour operators often offer short boat rides from the jetty, providing scenic views of the stilt hoes and the George Town coastline from the sea.The cultural and religio heart of the community is marked by its temples. There are two significant Chinese temples on Chew Jetty. The temple near the entrance is dedicated to the God of Heaven (Jade Deity), and another temple at the very end of the main jetty is dedicated to the Taoist Sea Deity, Haisen, to ensure good fortune and safe journeys at sea. While the primary temples follow Taoist and traditional Chinese folk religion, a prominent, striking structure adjacent to the Chew Jetty is the beautiful Hean Boo Thean Kuan Yin Temple, often referred to as the Floating Temple. While this temple is not physically on Chew Jetty, its stunning location perched right on the water makes it a major highlight when viewing the jetties from the coast or on a boat tour.
Chew Jetty's history began in 1888 when vario clans including the Tans and Lims, settled along Georgetown's waterfront, building hoes on stilts over the water creating unique clan communities. …

It was my first visit to Chew Jetty and totally loved it. I decided to visit during the evening as the sun was setting. The commercial shops were already closed or were closing up when I got there. It was not crowded at that time and I felt it was an enjoyable visit then. There were many hoes and shops on stilts along the Jetty.Some tips:- As there are residents who live along the jetty, the visiting hours are from 9am to 9pm. There is no cost to visit Chew Jetty.- Evenings work out to be the better time to visit as it is cooler besides having lesser crowds then.- There is a chinese taoist temple very close to the entrance of the jetty. It is free to visit and it is a beautifully, well maintained temple. Worth checking it out. (I have a separate review for this temple).- It is not the longest or largest jetty but probably the most visited by tourists.- A visit to Chew Jetty without patronising the shops but to see and take photos/ videos, should take between half hour to an hour top. It is really up to you as to how long you would like to spend time here.- When visiting, always keep a lookout for motorcyclists as they may ride by you to get in and out of their residences on the jetty.This is definitely a mt visit spot in my humble opinion. I fully appreciate why this is a UNESCO heritage site and jt beautiful to spend time here.

In one word, this perience was magical. And that was becae of this amazing combination of the location, the activity and the most incredible host, Mr Goh. I would like to cover each one separately.

The location - it is at the countryside in an area called Balik Pulau. It is quite far from Penang City, from areas like Georgetown. It will take almost 45-50 minutes to drive down. But, the way is beautiful and it is worth it to travel this far for this perience. The area is full of paddy fields and sparse population. You get to perience the real countryside whereby you see farmers haesting paddy.

The activity - it is an adventure like no other on bicycles. Mr Goh, takes you first to a cycle renting place. Here we chose 2 X double riding bicycles. One was for me and my wife. And the other was for my two daughters, 18 years and 10 years of age. My wife doesn't know how to cycle, but yet both of  managed beautifully. Yes, there is quite a bit of cycling to be done for almost 1 hour in total, but not continuoly. With enough stops and rests. Only in the evening while coming back to the starting point after sunset, it requires quite a bit of cycling and you might get a bit tired. But, nothing's worth it without a bit of hard work and adventure. And this tour is worth everything. So, after picking up your bicycles, you are taken to the paddy fields to perience paddy haesting. You cycle through a mix of concrete and gravel roads, but all easy to ride on. Mr Goh who accompanies you everywhere also on a cycle, will take many pictures of you with lovely backgrounds and great posing ideas. This lasts for almost an hour before you start moving towards 2 sunset points nt to the sea. Here you have to do a bit of trekking and reach those end points like places which overlook the setting sun on the sea. It is beautiful. The sunset is wonderful to watch and perience. You spend another hour before you ride back to the starting point which takes about 20 minutes of non-stop cycling. Once you deposit back your cycles, it is dark already. Mr Goh then takes you for dinner at a local street food place which is fantastic. He rides on his motorbike and you follow him. It took  half an hour to reach the dinner place which is an area with full of food stalls and in between are many tables to sit and enjoy fantastic local food. Mr Goh does all the ordering after doing a check on your preferred diet. The fried kway teow is simply fantastic. And then the satay. And then the coconut apom. Finally you finish dinner and say your goodbyes to Mr Goh. Few things to keep in mind. The tour starts at 5 pm and finishes around 9.30 pm after which you have to drive back wherever you are staying. Please wear sports shoes. Also eat something before you start as you will feel very hungry in between the tour. Wear clothes which cover your body and protect you from mosquitoes. If you’ve ever wondered what it’s like to step into a postcard that’s been left out in the sun a little too long—come visit Kampung Warna-Warni. You’ll find seven kampungs stitched together with bridges, murals, recycled art, and homes that look like they’ve been dipped in rainbow sherbet. Some are stunning. Others are, sadly, losing the battle against the UV rays. It can get quite hot, depending on the time of day—the kind of heat that makes you question why you’re not jt watching a travel vlog from your sofa. Most locals stay indoors becae of it, only coming out when it cools down.Don’t pect too many warm greetings—you might get a few curio looks, but that’s about it. It’s not that people are unfriendly; you’re simply walking through someone’s actual neighbourhood, and it feels like it. It’s a bit like Penang’s Clan Jetties, but rougher around the edges. You’re never quite sure whether the locals are pleased about the foot traffic or simply tolerating it.Parts of the village are well kept, while others clearly need some love, with pools of black, stagnant water filled with discarded plastic bottles and an unpleasant smell. What is undeniably beautiful is the view from the bridge connecting the village to the Kuala Perlis jetty—sweeping river views and a breeze in your face.There are a few stalls dotted around, and an open-air restaurant at the car park if you’re desperate for food or shade—nothing fancy, but you can grab some local snacks like shrimp fritters or dodol pulut hitam. Prices are fair, and the food hits the spot.Unless you enjoy slow-cooking yourself on foot, you can also pay RM35 and hop on a choley (a local motor-tricycle). There’s quite a lot to see, and it’s the easiest way to plore. The drivers know their way around and will point out interesting spots. Don’t pect National Geographic-style commentary—more like, “This aunty makes the best dodol.”It’s hard to make up your mind about this place. If Kampung Warna-Warni wants to be a proper tourist spot, it probably needs better upkeep—and perhaps a 7-Eleven or two—though I’m also not sure the residents would be too thrilled about their home being gentrified.

🌿 A Hidden Gem in Cameron Bertam Valley – Cozy Leisure Farm 🌄 My recent visit to Cozy Leisure Farm was nothing short of magical. Nestled approximately 1200m above sea level, the journey up the steep hill was thrilling in itself—an adventure that set the tone for the rest of the perience. The moment we arrived, we were greeted by crisp, cooling air and panoramic views that made the climb absolutely worth it. What truly made the visit special was the founder himself, whose warmth and hospitality were unmatched. He personally walked  through his estate and campsite, sharing stories of how he cultivated the land over the past 30 years. His passion was evident in every corner of the farm—from the flourishing avocado trees to the vibrant grapes, passion fruits, yellow golden fruits, and even robta and arabica coffee plants. It felt like stepping into a living, breathing encyclopedia of tropical agriculture. The farm also features two beautifully designed camping sites, perfect for a weekend escape into nature. Whether you’re a seasoned camper or jt looking to unplug, the serene atmosphere and fresh mountain air make it an ideal retreat. To top it all off, the owner seed  a delightful fruit platter, showcasing the very produce grown on his land. It was a sweet and refreshing end to an already unforgettable perience. And for families with children, there’s even a mini zoo with rabbits and birds, adding a playful and educational touch to the visit. If you’re looking for a unique, nature-filled getaway with a personal touch, Cozy Leisure Farm is a mt-visit. 🌱🍇🍍🐇🕊️

I recently organised a company session at Cwoks Batik Workshop last Saturday (26/8/2023)  and I mt say, it was an incredible perience from start to finish! The workshop was well-organized, informative, and a lot of fun.The instructor's passion for batik was evident as they guided  through the history, techniques, and creative process of batik making. Even as a beginner with no prior perience in batik, I felt comfortable and supported throughout the entire workshop.The workshop space was clean, well-lit, and had all the necessary materials neatly set up for . The instructor took their time plaining each step, making sure everyone was on the same page before moving forward. I appreciated the hands-on approach, and the instructor was patient in answering all our questions.The highlight of the workshop was definitely the hands-on batik creation. The instructor provided vario designs to choose from, but also encouraged  to let our creativity flow and design our own patterns. It was a therapeutic and rewarding process, and I was amazed at the end result.By the end of the workshop, I not only had a beautiful piece of batik art to take home but also a newfound appreciation for the art form. The entire perience was enriching and enjoyable. I highly recommend the Cwoks Batik Workshop to anyone interested in learning about batik or ploring their creative side. Whether you're a beginner or have some perience, this workshop is a great way to dive into the world of batik with a knowledgeable and friendly instructor.

Terletak di Gelang Patah, Kampung Sungai Melayu ialah sebuah perkampungan Melayu kecil yang kebanyakannya diduduki oleh 100% orang Melayu yang hidup sebagai nelayan. Kampung ini terletak di titik paling selatan negeri Johor, berhampiran dengan sempadan Singapura. Mengikut sejarah, kampung ini beria kira-kira 100 tahun dan pernah digunakan sebagai tempat tumpuan Tentera Jepun untuk menyerang Singapura semasa perang.Kampung Sungai Melayu terletak di tengah-tengah hutan bakau, yang menjadikannya syurga bagi kehidupan burung dan penduduk bakau yang lain. Penggemar memerhati burung akan gembira untuk melihat pelbagai spesies seperti helang, bangau, kuntul, walet dan burung rajawali.Kampung Sungai Melayu juga syurga makanan laut segar seperti ikan, ketam, udang, kerang dan lain-lain. Penduduk kampung turut memproses pelbagai jenis makanan laut seperti ikan kering, belacan dan banyak lagi.Apa nak buat ?Aktiviti memerhati burung di hutan bakau.Bermalam di rumah penduduk tempatan melalui program Homestay.Nikmati pelayaran sungai dan menyertai penduduk tempatan mencari ketam.Melawat rumah Melayu tradisional ala JohorTonton permainan tradisional dan pembuatan kraftangan.

Air BnB periences are a way for travellers to step into the shoes of a local and to perience their way of life for a fleeting moment. 

Fuad’s 3-hour perience of Kampung Baru delivers this and more. Essentially, it is an in-depth and personalised perience which is, at a visceral level, an perience which will transcend language, culture and religions. You will start the tour as strangers and leave as part of his keluarga (family).

Fuad is a 3rd generation resident of the kampung, and has witnessed first-hand the manner in which modernism has impacted the quaint kampung life. He will bring you through the kampung providing information and insights which no travel book will provide. My wife and I found ourselves snacking on freshly picked fruits - rambutan, guava and jambu, and were given a literal taste of life there.

The best part of this journey was to be given an opportunity to visit his home. There, we were welcomed by his wife, Kak Siti. We found ourselves spending the evening engaged in revelling conversations. We were also given an assortment of unique savoury snacks and kuihs, before we continued with the tour of the kampung. 

The evening ends with a dinner at nasi lemak wanjo, which is arguably thy national dish of Malaysia. The setting is no-frills, but the place is hygienic and the food is delectable. I will recommend the rendang ayam (chicken which is slow cooked in herbs and spices with a special coconut blend).

It was truly a memorable night and we can’t thank these wonderful people enough. Terima Kasih Fuad and Kak Siti.

Highly recommended shop in Kuala Terengganu to get high quality, authentic Terengganu songket (traditional cloth hand-woven with gold or silver thread). Available here are “sampin” which is a piece of cloth worn with the Malay “baju Melayu” on festive or formal events and songket cloth which can be made into traditional Malay women’s “baju kurung”. Your purchase here supports the local Terengganu artisans and keeps this traditional craft alive.

Update 8/12/2022Kami mahu cuba pasemboq yang telah ramai syorkan Padang Kota Lama. Kawasan makan ini seperti medan selera biasa (terdapat beberapa gerai makan lain di dalam satu bangunan). Jika anda sudah duduk di mana2 meja, pelayan akan datang serbu untuk ambil pesanan tapi di ingatkan untuk memesan pasembur perlu beratur di kedainya.Pasemboq yang disyorkan di medan selera ini adalah Gani Femes Pasembur dan Hin Pasembur. Kami pilih untuk cuba Gani Pasembur dahulu kali ini. Kuah pasemboq nya pekat dan mempungai banyak kacang yang tidak dihancurkan sepenuhnya untuk memberi tekstur rangup. Cucurnya juga masih rangup walaupun agak terdedah dan setelah dicurahkan kuah.Selain itu kami juga mencuba kerang bakar. Gerainya bersebelahan dengan Gani Pasembur. Sos pencicah kerangnya menaikkan lagi rasa kerang bakar.Air pula kami pesan cendol. Bagi saya cendolnya sedap tapi masih belum dapat menandingi cendol di masjid MITC Melaka.Secara keseluruhan, terdapat berbagai lagi menu makanan yang dapat dihidang di medan selera ini seperti goreng2, laksa, kuey tiaw dll. Cuma menu utama yang perlu cuba disini adalah pasembur.

The Filipino Market in Kota Kinabalu is a wonderful place to visit.  It is divided into sections: handicrafts, fruits, native snacks and fresh seafood.  At night, a part of the market becomes a night food market.  The people in the food market are actually nice but can be phy.  But no need to fear, jt buy what you want.  Do not buy what you don't want.The set meals at the night food market are affordable. 35 MYR for 2 people.  You get 4 different dishes of shrimp, squid, fish and vegetables and 2 seings of rice.  It is really good for its price.If you order fresh seafood to be cooked, then it is very pensive.  You will be charged cost of seafood pl seice fee.  One dish can already cost you 70 MYR.So choose wisely.  People are not dishonest, they can help you choose.  Ask them how much everything costs before agreeing.The night food market can be smokey. It is muddy when in rains.  If you are picky about places, this is not for you.  Personally, i like its charm.

The handicraft market in Kota Kinabalu can be underwhelming if you’re seeking authentic, locally made crafts, as many of the items available are mass-produced imports. However, it is part of the larger market compl that includes vibrant sections for food, vegetables, and fruits, offering a variety of local produce and delicacies that reflect Sabah’s rich culture. If you have time, it’s still worth a visit for the overall atmosphere and the chance to sample traditional foods or purchase fresh, otic fruits.For those specifically interested in authentic handicrafts, consider visiting the Mari Mari Cultural Village or the Sabah Art Gallery, where you can find traditional crafts made by local artisans. Additionally, the Gaya Street Sunday Market is a great spot to plore for more unique, locally crafted souvenirs alongside a variety of other authentic products. These alternatives provide a better opportunity to support local artisans and perience genuine Sabahan culture.

Baik,🌊Percutian kali ini ke Kuala Terengganu terasa lebih istimewa kerana dapat mencuba pengalaman pertama menaiki bot di Jeti Pak Awang River Cruise 🚤. Sejak awal lagi, suasana di jeti sudah memberikan rasa tenang – angin sungai yang sejuk, pemandangan air yang luas, dan bot yang kemas serta selesa untuk penumpang.Apabila bot mula bergerak, perjalanan menyuri sungai ini benar-benar membuka mata tentang keindahan Kuala Terengganu dari sudut berbeza. Kami melalui beberapa lokasi ikonik seperti:✅ Masjid Kristal – indah dan berkilauan dari tepi sungai, betul-betul jadi mercu tanda bandar ini.✅ Taman Tamadun Islam – nampak jelas replika bangunan-bangunan Islam terkenal dari serata dunia.✅ Pulau Duyong & Pulau Raja – kampung tradisional di tepi sungai yang penuh suasana tenang.✅ Jambatan Angkat – pengalaman unik lalu di bawah jambatan moden ini sambil menikmati pemandangan sekitar bandar.Yang paling menarik perhatian ialah apabila melalui tiang jambatan yang dihiasi mural lukisan berwarna-warni. Ia bukan sahaja cantik, tetapi juga jadi tanda arah laluan bot yang kreatif dan penuh seni 🎨.Keseluruhan perjalanan terasa santai, sesuai untuk keluarga, pasangan mahupun pelancong yang ingin melihat sisi lain Kuala Terengganu. Dengan bayaran yang berpatutan, pengalaman ini memang sangat berbaloi.💡 Tip tambahan: Kalau boleh, pilih waktu petang atau senja. Suasana matahari terbenam di sepanjang sungai menjadikan pengalaman ini lebih romantik dan menenangkan. 🌅Secara jujurnya, kami sekeluarga rasa sangat puas hati. Inilah aktiviti yang wajib dicuba bila datang ke Kuala Terengganu – bukan sahaja dapat tenangkan fikiran, tetapi juga menambah memori indah percutian.

I spent 3 days and two nights with host Mrs. Jubaidah and families at the beginning of May. They provided a warm and friendly environment for all their guests. The food was maello with something new to try at every meal. The catfish and river prawns were lovely as were the noodles and chicken .... It was lovely to be able to relax and get a feel for life in the village on the river. With our guides we saw a wonderful variety of birds: at least 4 species of hornbill, an asian paradise flycatcher, kingfishers, eagles and many more. We saw an orang-outang, probosc monkeys and silver langur and many macaques. We also came across evidence of elephants. It was a great perience.

If you want to truly authentic perience with the original people of the Kinabatangan River and support their community, you want to stay with the Bilit Village Homestay.  It is a wonderful community based program supporting 12 families that participate equally.  There is no benefit to staying with one family over another as they are all wonderful people and share in this biness endeavor equally as they do in so many parts of their lives.  We stayed with Hamdah who was like a sweet mother to  and her hband, Leam was our river guide. The homestay was basic, mattress on the floor with mosquito nets but spotless, amazing food locally sourced where possible.  We only did one night but wish we had done two as the girls that also stayed their second night with  saw pygmy elephants which isn't an everyday occurrence.  We had late afternoon and morning river boat cruises and Leam was incredibly obseant and patient. However,  if you have a lot of time available and curio to find out what the hype online is all about (like  :)) You won't be upset that you visited, but can't rule out coming back disappointed.Few tips:1. Get yourself mosquito repellent liquids prior to visiting (it is also sold there for RM 20 to 30) not so good, but does the job.2. Dress easy - this is not a boat or a ferry ride.. it's a catamaran ride. Be prepared for a bit of stretching and crouching.3. Carry a DSLR instead of relying on your phone. It basically boating in the night. You need something that can handle treme low light photography.4. The life vests are available in plenty but smelly (carry a body spray or perfume) otherwise you're only thinking of the odour/sweat smells and not enjoying the perience - You will for sure thank me for this one when you're wearing the vest. (You can hit that like in advance ;))5. Most important - Cards not accepted, jt ensure you have RM 50 available per boat of 4 adults6. Get there early, queue up outside the ticket counter. If not you'll end up in a long queue later.7. If you're in a group, split your team - Few can queue at the ticketing counter and the rest can queue up at the boarding area. So you don't end up queueing twice.The drive from KL is enjoyable, a mix of highways and fields/crops.Enjoy your trip!!!

Kek Lapis Dayang Salhah in Kuching, Sarawak, has earned its reputation as one of the city's renowned and highly recommended sellers of kek lapis, and it happens to be a favorite in my family as well. On this trip, I had the pleasure of introducing my friends to this cherished establishment.Our stay at the Hilton Hotel required  to cross the Sarawak River via a small boat, or sampan. Conveniently, the pier was jt a stone's throw away from the hotel. The short five-minute journey came at the minimal cost of RM1 per person, making it an enjoyable perience in itself.After disembarking on the other side of the river, we embarked on a brief five-minute walk to reach Kek Lapis Dayang Salhah. Arriving late in the evening around 9:30 PM, we found that most of the cakes were already sold out.Our group decided to sample some of the cakes and planned to place our orders the nt day. The prices here were considerably more affordable than those at the waterfront, and the taste was equally delightful, if not better. The butter-based kek lapis was priced slightly higher at RM25 per piece, while the margarine-based kek lapis cost RM15 per piece. For convenience, 5 to 6 kek lapis could be neatly packed into a small box that fit perfectly in a luggage bag. I ended up ordering two boxes.What truly stood out was their ceptional ctomer seice. Kek Lapis Dayang Salhah offered delivery directly to our hotel, with no additional charge. We only had to pick up our orders at the hotel lobby. For larger orders ceeding 15 pieces, it's advisable to place the order a day in advance to ensure the cakes are freshly baked and promptly delivered.My personal favorite has always been the masam manis kek lapis, and I found it jt as delightful as ever. Without a doubt, I'll be ordering from Kek Lapis Dayang Salhah again in the future, and I would highly recommend it to anyone seeking a taste of Sarawak's famo kek lapis.

Probably one of the best layer cake or kuih lapis shop in Kuching. This is their main store and many visitors come here to buy these, even locals. The prices here are lower than what is sold in the city. The shop is located a little inside from the main road, so will need to walk down a short path. Footwear not allowed inside. Kek Lapis Warisan is the go-to spot for layered cake (kek lapis) in Kuching. Located jt about 3.5 km from Kuching Waterfront, it is popular among both locals and tourists looking to buy souvenirs. Visitors can watch the live preparation …

Kek Lapis Warisan memang terbaik. Pilihan kek banyak, rasa sedap dan tak terlalu manis. Tekstur lembut, potongan kemas dan nampak berkualiti.Harga berbaloi dengan kualiti. Beli 10 percuma 1. Beli 20 percuma 3. Paling menarik, pelanggan boleh saksikan demo cara-cara membuat kek lapis secara langsung – pengalaman yang unik dan menyeronokkan. Sesuai jika nak beli sebagai buah tangan atau makan sendiri. Memang antara kedai kek lapis yang wajib singgah kalau ke Sarawak

Kek Lapis Warisan Sdn Bhd in Kuching is renowned for its premium Sarawak layer cakes, offering over 30 flavors, including Hati Pari, Cadbury, and Cheezy Oreo. Visitors can obsee the baking process firsthand, enhancing their perience. The shop plans to pand with a second branch in Kota Samarahan. Prices start at RM10 per cake.

Saya berpeluang mencuba kek lapis Sarawak dari booth Kek Lapis Warisan ini semasa menghadiri satu pertandingan dan saya berasa teruja dengan pengalaman tersebut. Rasa kek lapis yang ditawarkan amat sedap dan memikat, dengan pelbagai pilihan perisa yang unik serta memuaskan. Daripada banyak pilihan yang ada, dua perisa yang paling menambat selera saya ialah kek lapis perisa Hati Pari dan Belacan, tekstur dan gabungan rasa yang sedikit berbeza. Kedua-duanya mempunyai rasa yang kaya, seimbang antara manis dan buah-buahan, serta tekstur yang lembut tetapi padat – Rasa original dan authentic kek lapis Sarawak yang asli.Seis Dan layanan yang diberikan di booth ini mesra dan very helpful untuk say memilih kek lapis yang kena dengan selera saya. Booth mereka dibuka secara strategik di lokasi pertandingan, menjadikan proses mencuba dan membeli kek lapis sangat mudah tanpa perlu berjalan jauh. Saya sangat menghargai penyediaan ‘tester’ yang banyak dan tanpa had – pengunjung bebas mencuba pelbagai jenis kek selama booth masih dibuka. Ini memberi peluang untuk memilih dengan yakin tanpa rasa ragu.Apa yang menambahkan lagi keselesaan pengalaman ini ialah keramahan sales coordinator mereka. Mereka sangat membantu, memberi cadangan perisa mengikut citarasa pelanggan, serta tidak jemu menjawab soalan dengan senyuman. Ini menunjukkan komitmen tinggi dalam memberikan perkhidmatan pelanggan yang berkualiti.Secara keseluruhan, saya amat berpuas hati dengan kualiti kek, layanan mesra, serta pendekatan mesra pelanggan oleh pasukan di booth ini. Kek lapis Sarawak mereka bukan sahaja sesuai untuk dinikmati sendiri, malah amat sesuai dijadikan hadiah atau buah tangan dari Borneo. Saya pasti akan kembali membeli lagi bila-bila say datang ke Sarawak semula 👍

I have been buying this 'keropok lekor' from another famo shop for years. This time I was introduced to this shop by a colleague working around here. A pretty simple village style shop and they sell dried fish crackers as well. The fried lekor was especially delicio, very suitable to my taste compared to the other shops I've been buying from. You can have them in vacuum sealed packets whether in fried or braised forms especially if you want to take them on the plane. The only downside is that they don't offer packing in boxes like other famo shops in case you are buying in bulk and have them as checked baggage. But hey, you can still hand-carry them onto the plane in the plastic bags or your backpack.

Setiap kali datang ke Kuala Terengganu, terutamanya di Kampung Losong, saya pasti memilih makanan tempatan berasaskan ikan.Kali ini, saya singgah di Kedai Tokku Leko, yang menjual keropok lekor dan sata. Keropok lekor gorengnya penuh dengan rasa ikan, dan apabila dicicah dengan sos cili, rasanya semakin sedap. Skala: 3.8/5Namun, bagi saya, makanan paling istimewa di sini adalah sata ikan. Rasanya sangat enak, dengan keseimbangan lemak dan pedas yang sempurna. Ini adalah sata ikan paling sedap yang pernah saya cuba. Skala: 4.5/5Whenever I visit Kuala Terengganu, especially Kampung Losong, I always opt for local seafood-based dishes.This time, I went to Kedai Tokku Leko, which sells keropok lekor and sata. The fried keropok lekor had a rich fish flavor, and when dipped in chili sauce, it became even more delicio. Rating: 3.8/5However, for me, the true highlight here is the sata ikan. The taste is absolutely delightful, with a perfect balance of creaminess and spiciness. This is, without a doubt, the best sata ikan I have ever had. Rating: 4.5/5

We came here in February. The place is located across the river, and this is a single accom hut in the farm - so you’re basically the only ones there - which is why it’s so cool! At night it can get pretty eerie as there were no one else there in the viccinity but we had a Mr. Wton who camped outside during our stay so he was always there to make sure we were OK. The river in front of the hut - superb! It’s not rocky like in Kolumpong or Tegudon and best thing is - the river is all yours for the entire stay. We enjoyed our stay here - it is one of a kind perience. No TV, wifi is weak, and there are bugs flying around especially at night so you may want to keep your sleeves long and bring a bug repellent. Get a full board package so you don’t have to cook your own meals pl you’re helping the community to earn some cash.

Koisaan Cultural Village (KCV) at KDCA is a fantastic place to immerse yourself in the rich cultural heritage of the Kadazan-Dun people. The cultural performances are vibrant and engaging, giving visitors a genuine taste of the local traditions. The village is well-maintained, and the hospitality is outstanding. Highly recommended for anyone interested in learning about Sabah's indigeno cultures and enjoying a unique, enriching perience.

Anyway, here's what I liked the most, the handmade handicrafts, the traditional cultural longhoe (RM1.00 per entrance), the homemade delicacies like lihing, sikat, fish and fruit pickles ranging from RM10.00 and above (no free sample/food testing okay), the local vendors' agro culture products (sadly, I didn't see much of it, since the name of  'Kaamatan' means haest clearly mentioned) but I enjoyed the Goat Milk Honey Ice Cream though (yummy *.^). For a first timer visit to KDCA during Haest Festival, it will be a cultural shock, where you can see lots of 'pork' and alcohol stalls here and there pl the locals get drunk till morning (I wondered how the nearby neighborhood can sleep at night with the 'nightclub' noises) Thanks God it was a sunny day. For your information, the entrance fee (not on festival season) is RM25.00 for local and Foreigner is RM35.00. Here are my snapshots on the Kaamatan event itself *.^

Went there on the morning of 19 April.At 10pl in the morning, the sun was killing .The cultural village tour was fruitful and we got nice periences in it.We bought the ticket at the location. we think we were the first visitor to the compound on that day.We visited the vario model hoes of the natives.But the memorable peak of visiting this place will be the station tasting Lihing (glutino rice wine), eating sago grubs (butod), learning to start fire and cook the butod, e the blowpipes, jumping onto the traditional trampoline and watching the traditional mic being played on gongs and kenong.Our guide, a young lady (wished I could have asked for her name) was very pleasant and knowledgeable. We asked a lot of questions beyond what was seen onsite.The murut hoe is citing as we have to cross the swinging bridge, to be met by the native from Murut on the other end.The sago grubs, or Butod is a MT TO TRY!!Yes, initially the thought of eating a worm can really test ones nees. But after overcoming it, the butod eating is actually pleasant and delectable.There is nothing unfamiliar with the taste, cept for the fear of eating a worm.The butod personally for me tasted like fried egg. Especially the white part of the egg white which was fried with a bit of brown bits on it. Some said it tasted like fresh uncooked sweet corn.Becae the flavour is familiar, I am compelled to say that I am definitely going to have more butod in restaurants around KK that sees them.I do recommend tourists to visit this place, even though the weather is not friendly enough. Becae their hospitality is superb!Wishing KDCA all the best and will pand to have more native hoing and share more of the native cultures and history with . Will visit you again definitely!!??

If Google tells you the ticket costs RM200, don’t panic. It’s actually closer to RM25. Some private charters may charge more, but the standard tour is affordable and easy to join. The boats are clean, the life jackets are in good condition, and the whole trip has a “choose your own adventure” feel. Some ads mention dolphin sightings, but don’t count on it—they’re seasonal at best, maybe even mythical. No one brought them up on our visit.What is on offer is a surprisingly rich view of river life. You’ll pass under the town’s iconic bridge while someone plains the different types of handmade boats. Then you’ll stop at a kelong (a floating fish farm)—part zoo, part classroom—where you might hold a horseshoe crab or gently touch a pufferfish. Kids get cited. Adults act cool. But everyone learns something, whether they admit it or not.The highlight is the eagles—and they don’t disappoint. They are trained and arrive when the boats do. Like clockwork, the sky fills with 20 to 30 pairs of wings. Rumour has it you get to feed them, but we didn’t on our trip. Still, they fly close enough for great photos—and even better memories.Most staff speak Malay and Mandarin. English varies depending on the guide. If your guide is Khairul, you’re in luck—he’s passionate, knowledgeable, and deeply respectful of the environment. The kind of person who makes you care about mangrove trees more than you thought possible.Local schoolchildren often help out with ctomer seice. Some are enthiastic, others clearly wish they were somewhere else. On the one hand, it’s impressive how capable they are. On the other, you hope they’re being treated well and not skipping school jt to help tourists hold sea creatures. Either way, it gives a glimpse into the town’s effort to keep eco-tourism alive.It gets by during peak times—pect long queues and less of a personal feel. Visit early or during off-peak hours if you prefer something quieter. Tours last around an hour to 90 minutes. Timings are flible—or jt loosely followed. Some slots are fixed, but others seem to leave when enough people arrive. It’s charming in a slightly chaotic, agak-agak kind of way.All in all, this isn’t a polished theme park—and it doesn’t try to be. It’s the whole kampung coming together to share what it knows, what it loves, and what it’s trying hard to protect. And if you’re lucky, you’ll leave with more than jt pictures of pufferfish—you’ll leave with a new perspective.

On 6.6.2025, our family embarked on a memorable river boat tour in Kuala Sepetang, Taiping that offered an enriching blend of nature, wildlife, and local culture. Tickets were reasonably priced at RM25 per person. We were the first group to set off that morning at 10:00 AM, joined by three other families about 12 people in total.The tour began with a warm welcome from the locals, who were enthiastic and eager to share their knowledge. Our guide, a friendly and knowledgeable young Chinese girl, accompanied by an perienced boatman, made the journey even more engaging with fascinating stories about Kuala Sepetang’s history and environment.Our first stop was a floating fish farm, where we got up close with a variety of marine life, including crabs, puffer fish, and several other sea creatures. We had the chance to feed them and snap some close-up photos, making it a fun and educational activity for both kids and adults.From there, the boat took  along a peaceful stretch of mangrove forest. Gliding through the calm waters, we soaked in the beauty of the mangrove ecosystem an important natural habitat rich in wildlife and tranquillity.The final part of the tour was particularly citing. As the boatman gave a few loud honks in the middle of the river, dozens of wild eagles appeared and began circling above . Watching them glide and dive overhead was a spectacular sight perfect for photography and an unforgettable end to the journey.In summary, the river tour was an unforgettable perience that delighted the whole family. Between the close encounters with marine life, the scenic cruise through mangroves, and the incredible sight of wild eagles responding to the boat’s honking, it’s a mt do activity for anyone visiting Kuala Sepetang. Highly recommended!

Our tour guide was En.Khairul, who is very knowledgeable about the local history and ecosystem of Kuala Sepetang. His tour is different from the other commercial tour operators in the sense that 1) his tour group size is smaller so you get a more personalised perience, and 2) he is more sensitive to the needs of the local flora and fauna, and will not encourage any activities that will harm it. His tour was also slower in pace, so we didn't feel rhed and were able to take our time ploring each stop. And he brought  pretty close to the fireflies, which was amazing and something we noticed the commercial tour boats were not able to do. Truly a memorable trip with En.Khairul.

Stumbled upon this private tour seice on Google Map while searching for activities to do in Taiping for a family trip. In contact personally with Mr. Khairul Salleh. Since it was a private tour seice, time was flible - which is a pl for a crowd with kids, trying to get ready on time!Mr. Khairul Salleh is very friendly yet professional, and very knowledgeable in regards to Kuala Sepetang and its neighbourhood.We took 2 trip packages: daytime trip around Kuala Sepetang & nighttime trip for the fireflies.Daytime Kuala Sepetang trip:90min boat trip along the river. We were shown around looking at the fishing indtry, the mangrove forest & the coal factory. We landed at the 'Shell Beach' where the kids managed to collect vario seashells washed ashore by the local cockle & shellfish indtries byproducts. Then we stopped at the fish farm looking at tilapias, horseshoe crabs, pufferfish & mudskippers. Info sharing session by Mr. Khairul Salleh was very informative & interactive. I was very happy that my siblings & the kids were very happy getting involved throughout the trip. We ended the trip watching eagles feeding on the river. Magnificent! Oh, almost forgot the mangrove sapling release into the river with hope of everlasting green ecosystem for the future to enjoy & cherish!Nighttime firefly trip:Another memorable trip. One hour session watching fireflies amongst the mangrove trees flashing lights synchronoly along the river. We were all in awe by Mr. Khairul Salleh's vast knowledge of the fireflies & the mangrove habitat, and reminded  how crucial & important keeping earth clean & green is to the stainability of the ecosystem.All in all, we really enjoyed it & definitely would recommend it! Keep it up, En. Khairul!!!

You also get a chance to view different types of coastal birds in the area. There is a stopover at a kelong where you get to feed the fish and also get a chance to hold puffer fish and horse shoe crabs. There is also a cool demo of archer fish hunting their "prey". This attraction foces on edible bird's nest farming. Visitors are introduced to the vario processes of farming and traction of swiftlet nest and is very educational especially for the young children. There is also a bird’s nest farm with a small gallery for visitors to plore. Additionally, the farm has a café and restroom facilities. Given the limited attractions in Tanjung Sepat, this can be a convenient stop for those looking for a brief visit.

This is an interesting eco park of 5 acres, engaged in swiftlet farming and bird nest processing. Beside the large car park, there is a row of six antique cars with the year of make and photographs of before and after refurbishment.At the bird nest processing centre, you can watch through a large glass wall, the workers bily cleaning the bird nests. The centre looks like a laboratory with the workers all dressed in blue uniform. At this centre, you can find bird nest, honey and some health food for sale. Behind this is a café that sees food with bird nest. A few steps away from the café is another sales centre dealing with mainly organic cereals.On the ground floor of the swiftlet hoe, you can watch a video presentation of everything about bird nest and a mock-up of the swiftlet hoe. You can see real bird nest attached at the ceiling but the swiftlets are fake. The entrance fee is RM3. Beside the swiftlet hoe is the mini zoo. The entrance fee is RM5 (3/10/16).

Had a fantastic time with Langkawi Eco Ventures and cannot recommend them enough! Our guide Sham was all in all everything you want in a guide: super fun, engaging, knowledgeable and well prepared. Overall did not know what to pect when booking the perience but came away feeling fulfilled by a good morning of learning!We started the day around 10am (there were clear instructions on how to meet the guide). We gathered and first took a boat to a floating platform which turned out to be a restaurant. There we already got the opportunity to get up close and personal with some of the marine life in the geoforest park, including horseshoe crabs and stingrays. After that we grabbed our gear, hats and water prepared by the guide, and plored the mangroves on kayak over a good 2-3 hours. The time absolutely flew by and while it may sound like a long time, we had ample rest stops where Sham shared his perience and answered our questions about the mangroves. By the end of the morning we had seen different types of marine life, learnt about the local monkeys, and saw several eagles! Around 1:30pm we had the opportunity to share lunch with the new friends made and even got lucky and snuck a visit to the Bat Cave nearby before we ended! The guide, boatmen and other members of the group were really friendly and outgoing.Would really recommend this company to go with, if you’re looking to do something to learn more about the natural environment in Langkawi and get some ercise in the meantime! Guest perience is very important to him therefore his policy is to keep group sizes small (max 10).We then took a speed boat into the Eco Forrest. The first stop was the bat cave which was fascinating. Sham took  through the cave and we saw the bats up close. (Beware the trouble making monkeys).After, we got back in the speed boat and headed to the floating restaurant where we picked up our Kayaks. Sham provided  with water and dry bags. I thought I could at least enjoy an perience in this place since one has to pay an entrance fee of RM30 for local tourists and RM40 for overseas tourists.The welcome drink was jt iced cordial near the front desk. Each guest was given a weaved headgear to wear. There was not much of activities cept watching one of the orang asli caing from a tree trunk (which was the only highlight). If you are pecting a dance or anything of that sort, there was none. Either there were too few tourists or it was too early (we were there at 11.30am)There was no guide to give you a personal feel of the lifestyle of the orang asli. You are practically on your own walking and entering the galleries (which you need to e your handphone torchlight to read the history, as the lighting was very dim).There were two ladies waiting near the cafe to teach tourists some simple weaving/origami to take home.To make it a little more worthwhile, I had my guests play "5 stones" at the cafe, and shoot seeds with catapults.It was at least educational and to see the unique and intricate caings.

I walked into this cultural centre hoping to learn about Malaysia’s orang asli but I came away learning more.Mr Rashid, his dedication and passion to this place and their people desees our utmost respect, showed  around.He painstakingly plained Mah Meri people’s history and their practices.To see this place, it’s preferred that you book in advance as they have limited guides.Mah Meri are proud and happy to receive visitors with a welcoming ceremony , blessing and even a crown.They will prepare according to the informed numbers and requests.It’s a small place but, only thru a guide’s planation will you be able to learn their history properly. It’s interesting that they already know and was practicing what we are only jt finding out and starting to teach our young ones now.There are only about 4200 of them. They need our support to remain as part of Malaysia’s colorful history.
Interesting place to know orang asli named Mah Meri or people that live in jungle. Not that far from KL, and can continue the trip to Morib.

It was a very pleasant and casual 2-hour perience. We had the opportunity to understand and learn more about Mah Meri's cultures by chatting with a lady, who is the person in charge of the cultural village.She is very well-versed and communicated well in English with . She singlehandedly trained the female employees, posed them with the responsibility of maintaining and promoting their identity, in the cultural village. So that the world knows such amazing cultures and beliefs a minor has.There are notable spots where maintenance is needed, such as air-conditioning and pavements, and the whole place is currently run by their own, which means they are not subsidized or sponsored by the Malaysian government. Hence, any help from the public is needed. Let's help them together!

Malaysia Heritage Studio is an ceptional destination that beautifully celebrates Malaysia's rich cultural tapestry. It offers a unique perience, allowing visitors to immerse themselves in the diverse heritage of the nation. From traditional crafts and intricate batik designs to mesmerizing cultural performances, the studio sees as a living meum that showcases Malaysia's vibrant traditions.The ambiance is warm and welcoming, making it a perfect place for both locals and tourists to plore the roots of Malaysia's multicultural identity. The knowledgeable staff enhances the perience with engaging stories and detailed planations, providing insights into the history and significance of each hibit.Moreover, the studio often organizes interactive workshops where visitors can learn skills like batik painting or traditional dance, making the visit both educational and memorable. Whether you're a history enthiast or someone looking for a meaningful cultural perience, Malaysia Heritage Studio is a mt-visit destination that truly embodies the heart and soul of Malaysia.

Malaysia Heritage Studios adalah destinasi yang sangat menarik dan berbaloi untuk dikunjungi, terutama bagi mereka yang ingin mengenal lebih dekat budaya dan warisan setiap negeri di Malaysia. Tempat ini menampilkan pelbagai rumah tradisional dari setiap negeri, lengkap dengan informasi yang disesuaikan untuk kanak-kanak yang bersekolah. Ini menjadikan pengalaman pembelajaran mereka lebih menarik dan interaktif. Setiap rumah tradisional disertai dengan sejarah dan latar belakang yang mudah difahami, menjadikannya destinasi yang sesuai untuk pelancongan pendidikan.Selain pameran rumah, terdapat juga aktiviti dan persembahan menarik yang boleh dinikmati oleh pengunjung. Ini membantu menghidupkan lagi suasana dan membolehkan pengunjung lebih mendalami budaya tempatan dengan cara yang interaktif.Walau bagaimanapun, salah satu penambahbaikan yang boleh dilakukan oleh pihak penguran ialah menggantikan patung-patung selebriti antarabangsa seperti Angelina Jolie, Brad Pitt, dan Mr Bean dengan tokoh-tokoh legenda Malaysia. Contohnya, P. Ramlee, Saloma, Dato' Siti Nurhaliza, dan Michelle Yeoh. Ini bukan sahaja dapat memperkenalkan ikon budaya dan seni Malaysia kepada pengunjung tempatan dan antarabangsa, tetapi juga membanggakan warisan negara kita sendiri.Secara keseluruhan, Malaysia Heritage Studios adalah tempat yang har dikunjungi oleh semua lapisan masyarakat, terutamanya bagi mereka yang ingin mendalami warisan Malaysia dalam suasana yang interaktif dan mendidik.

Free for children below 12 years old.It takes about an hour to visit the whole area.If you want to attend the culture event, then you have to go there only in weekends at 11 am and 4 pm.From Wikipedia "Mini Malaysia & ASEAN Cultural Park is a cultural park where impressive replicas of traditional Malaysian and ASEAN homes can be seen. Similar to the Karyaneka Handicraft Centre in KL, each hoe contains furnishings, fixtures and works of art depicting the culture of each state or country.Located in Ayer Keroh, the Mini Malaysia & ASEAN Cultural Park showcases the country's 13 states represented with hoes that actually look alike to the untrained eye (save for the Borneo hoe). However inside each abode is where the differences can be seen with life-sized wax dummies dressed in respective traditional costumes as well as local handicrafts. At the Mini Malaysia Compl there is also a model of an Orang Asli village."

Mari mari cultural village is a popular tourist attraction in Sabah Malaysia. It's a village that showcases the different ethnic groups in Sabah Malaysia. We avail the tour + meals.

Mari Mari Cultural Village is a unique place that allows you to immerse yourself in the rich culture of Sabah’s five main tribes: the Kadazan-Dun, Rung, Lundayeh, Bajau and Murut. Each of these tribes has its own unique history, traditions and lifestyle, which you can learn about during an interactive tour of the village. Visitors can see traditional hoes, learn how to make fire with bamboo, learn about tribal tattoos and try local delicacies.Transport and accessibilityGetting to the village is relatively easy ing the Grab app, which allows you to quickly book a ride from Kota Kinabalu. However, getting back can be more difficult – finding a driver in the area can often require a long wait. So it’s worth planning your visit in advance or considering an organised tour that includes round-trip transport.Is it worth visiting?Despite the minor inconvenience of the return journey, Mari Mari Cultural Village is a place worth visiting. It’s not jt a meum, but a living space where you can perience authentic Borneo culture and get a feel for the old days. If you are interested in the history and traditions of indigeno tribes, this place will surely provide an unforgettable perience.

Mari Mari Cultural Village offers a well-organised and thoughtful introduction to the traditional cultures of Sabah. The visit gives a good sense of how different indigeno groups lived, with the chance to see traditional homes, try local food and drinks, and learn about ctoms that are still respected today.Our guide for the English-speaking group, Zul, was calm, clear and informative. He guided  through the perience with patience and knowledge, creating a comfortable atmosphere without rhing or overloading .The whole Mari Mari team desees credit for their effort – everything was well prepared and ran smoothly, and it’s clear how much care and pride they put into their work. From the performances to the details in each hoe, you can see a real dedication to keeping these traditions alive and sharing them in a respectful way.A valuable perience for anyone interested in local culture, presented with sincerity and professionalism.

Mari Mari was an absolutely unforgettable perience! The views and huts were absolutely beautiful. Learning all about Sabah’s history and different tribes and groups was not only an amazing educational time, but it was also incredibly interactive. Our guide, Ben, did a wonderful job sharing stories and history as well as his sharing his own childhood stories which i loved hearing about. Everyone there was so kind and welcoming and I’m really going to miss it there. Of course we can’t forget about the show at the end - seeing all the traditional clothes and dances was so incredible i couldn’t stop smiling or look away.The Mari Mari cultural village made me fall in love with Sabah. Would 100% recommend and would definitely want to go back.

My visit to Mari Mari Cultural Village was one of the highlights of my trip to Kota Kinabalu. This place offers a beautifully curated and interactive glimpse into the diverse indigeno cultures of Sabah, all set within lh rainforest surroundings.From the moment we stepped into the village, we were welcomed warmly by our friendly and knowledgeable guide. The tour took  through several traditional hoes representing different tribes — the Dun, Rung, Lundayeh, Bajau, and Murut. Each home was brought to life with demonstrations and storytelling, from fire-starting and tattoo ink preparation to blowpipe techniques and traditional rice wine processes.I especially appreciated the opportunity to try Kuih Penjaram, a sweet local delicacy made with rice and pandan — soft, fragrant, and delicio! We also learned about native crafts and even sampled food cooked in bamboo. The Murut longhoe segment was fascinating — rich with history, culture, and even a bit of dramatic flair.The surroundings were serene, with a peaceful stream running through the village adding to the charm. This wasn’t jt a passive meum-style perience — it was hands-on, authentic, and respectful of the indigeno heritage.Highly recommended for anyone wanting to deepen their understanding of Sabah’s people and traditions. Allocate at least half a day, bring water, and wear comfortable shoes — you’ll be walking through the forest and up bamboo stairs. It’s worth every step!

Original Cheese Kek Lapis = RM10.00Most of  would know of this Indonesian Cake known as "Kueh Lapis" but in Kuching, its known as "Kek Lapis". This is another Kuching Speciality. This is the type of Cake that really takes alot of patience to make. Jt looking at the intricate layers upon layers and you can tell that alot of work is gone into the making.Kek Lapis in Kuching are ually sold in a Thick Long Slab and mostly range from RM8.00 to RM15.00 and up. And from most of the Kek Lapis shops I visited, the prices are more or less the same throughout.There are many Kek Lapis Shops around and I chose this one specifically becae they have 2 Adorable Cats within! They're so Adorable they're really Enjoying Themselves, Sleeping and Manja all over the place.The Staffs here at Maria are also very Friendly with a Smile and I chose their Classic Kek Lapis which is something called "Original Cheese" Of course there are many more flavours to choose from. There are so many Beautiful Variations of Kek Lapis, it almost feels like you're touring an Art Gallery. They even have a Free Sample station so you can have a try which Flavour you prefer before you make your decision.For this Original Cheese Flavour, the thing that I noticed first was that it had a very Strong Cheesecake like Smell. The Actual Cake don't actually have any Cheese Taste though, which was a slight disappointment, but the Butter taste is Very Prominent. Its not the Super Buttery type of Taste, but you can definitely feel its presence here. Personally, I also thought it tasted jt nice and not too sweet.After tasting this, now I understand why this is called "Kek Lapis" rather than "Kueh Lapis" becae this totally doesn't taste like how you'd imagine it to taste. It really does taste more like a Cake than a Kueh. Tture is Soft, Sticky and Spongy, and I thought it tend to stick to my Throat often. This is perfect if washed down with some Tea or Coffee.RM10 for this size of Kek Lapis I feel is worth it jt becae of how much work it takes to make this.Personally, I don't know if this is really the Best Kek Lapis ever, but the Combination of the Friendly Staffs, and the 2 Super Adorable Cats is a Yes from me for Maria. I can recommend to come here if ya'll want to give Kek Lapis a try.Thanks for Reading. Come join my Facebook Food Blog: "A Solo Singaporean's Guide to JB Foods" to view the Full Review with Photos.

Mira Cake Hoe ( Cawangan Keramat).Sapa rindukan Kek Lapis Sarawak Mira boleh mai sini.... Bermacam jenis kek lapis boleh dibeli sama mcm Ibu Pejabat di Sarawak..The Sarawak layer cake, known as kek lapis Sarawak (meaning "Sarawak layer cake") or kek lapis moden Sarawak in Malay, is a layered cake from the state of Sarawak in Malaysia.This cake can be found almost everywhere in the Malaysian state of Sarawak. ually Kek Lapis Sarawak will be seed on special occasions. They are often baked for religio or cultural celebrations such as Eid ul-Fitr, Christmas, Deepavali, Gawai, birthdays and weddings.People in Malaysia practice an open hoe tradition during any festival day, and the modern layered cakes way be seed almost everywhere in Sarawak.The Sarawak layer cake has its origin in a form of layer cake with vario spices found in Indonesia called lapis legit or kek lapis Betawi (Betawi refers to Batavia, the old name of Jakarta).This spiced Betawi cake is thought to have derived from a form of European spit cake, which was made by the wives of Dutch administrators in Batavia during the colonial period and seed during evening tea.It is believed that this type of spiced layer cake was introduced to Sarawak in the 1970s and 1980s by the Betawis from Jakarta. The Sarawak people then added new ingredients, flavour and colour that resulted in a new version of the layer cake being introduced and named as Sarawak layer cake. In addition, modern Sarawak layered cakes were inspired by Western cake-making in the early 1980s, later modern design to the traditional layer cake, along with new flavours were introduced.

Pendek kata, kalau korang craving kek lapis Sarawak, Mira Cake Hoe ni memang berbaloi untuk dicuba!

Mira Cake Hoe is one of the most popular spots in Kuching for buying Sarawak kek lapis (layer cake), and it definitely lives up to its reputation. The shop itself is bright, colorful, and welcoming, with rows of beautifully patterned cakes neatly displayed. Walking in, you immediately feel tempted to try everything becae of how attractive the designs and colors are.The variety here is cellent. They have all the classic flavors such as butter, chocolate, pandan, and cheese, but also many creative ones that you don’t often find elsewhere. What I really liked is that they allow you to sample different flavors before deciding which ones to buy. This makes it fun and also ensures you get actly what you enjoy.Taste-wise, the cakes are moist, rich, and flavorful, with jt the right level of sweetness. They are packed nicely in travel-friendly boxes, which makes them perfect for souvenirs. Prices are reasonable for the quality and craftsmanship, especially considering how labor-intensive making kek lapis is.The staff are friendly, patient, and always ready to plain the differences between the many flavors. Seice feels organized even when the shop gets by.Overall, Mira Cake Hoe is a mt-visit if you are in Kuching. Whether you want to bring home gifts, surprise friends, or jt enjoy authentic Sarawak layer cake, this place will not disappoint

Murug Turug Waterfalls, located in Minangkob, Tamparuli, is a hidden gem for hiking enthiasts. Opt for Trail C, and you'll be rewarded with an hilarating trek that features seven stunning waterfalls. The lh surroundings and pristine nature create a serene and captivating atmosphere. The highlight is the 5th waterfall, which boasts a beautiful and majestic flow, perfect for a well-deseed rest and photo opportunity.Overall, Murug Turug Waterfalls provides an unforgettable hiking perience, combining physical challenge with natural beauty. Ensure you're well-prepared with sturdy footwear, ample water, and snacks to fully enjoy this remarkable trail.

Went for the intermediate trail and hiked to 3 waterfalls. This tour is definitely a MT-GO if you pass by the area. The terrain was q challenging but nonetheless still doable. Did the tour on a weekday and practically had the waterfalls all to ourselves!

Went with a group of friends for the package C and package B [Package C was advertised as 8km - in actuality it was 12km, and Package B is about 4km or less] but to be honest, it wasn't that hard. I would say for me it was at least easy-medium level difficulty for package C and very easy for package B.But the waterfalls were clean and beautiful to say the least, and when we went there, it wasn't high-tide? What I meant by that is the water levels was as high as the second time I went [Went for Package B the second visit]. The guides were friendly and helpful for my group members who was less outdoor-inclined. They do provide Maggi-cup noodles at the 5th Waterfall [Murug-Turug] - but you'd have to pay for it. If I'm not mistaken, 1 maggi-cup noodle was about RM4. You can even rent floats at the 5th waterfall to enjoy in the waters.Other amenities they provided - shoes for rent at the starting point - but mind you it is what we Sabahan calls - Kasut Adidas Kampung. They also provide free e of hiking sticks made of wood.You can opt for certificates at the cost of RM5 per person.All in all, the whole booking process was cheap, and absolutely had no hassle whatsoever.

With very cheap and affordable prices as low as $10, you could get a guide pl a certificate at and additional $5 to hike towards the breathtaking view of Murug Turug waterfall. A double price will be charged for foreigners. There are three trails in total, the shortest and easiest trail being Trail A at 4 km. The guide is perienced and understanding, so do not worry about falling behind. We had a slight delay so I only wish they would start on time to avoid the hottest part of the day.
